degraded: The external CODE review returned only ONE usable leg. Gemini's response was a truncated internal-monologue fragment that began mid-sentence and cut off inside a code block, not findings -- the same degenerate mode seen on S2. Recorded rather than re-rolled so the thin code-review coverage stays visible. OpenAI's leg returned one medium finding (an epoch generated_at in the committed manifest), fixed by regenerating through the real update_compliance path. The PLAN review had BOTH legs substantive, and the coordinator's own review round found 6 further defects.

classify_complexity returned estimate=small with risk flags touches_rls + touches_migrations. Both are PROSE false positives -- the classifier matches the description text, not the diff; no RLS policy and no SQL migration is touched. Executed at the briefed medium level (external plan review AND code review cascade both ran). risk_detectors.py diff-predicates are authoritative and F11 recomputes.

unit=6719 is the SUM of the four suites re-run and verified for this change (shared/tests 4661, shipwright-compliance 1246, shipwright-adopt 427, shipwright-grade 385). The prior entry 4666 counted the shared suite alone; the wider number is recorded because this change edits fixtures in three plugins.

The internal reviewer cascade (spec-reviewer / code-reviewer / doubt-reviewer) is DELEGATED to the campaign orchestrator: this runner has no Agent tool. The orchestrator's code review returned 6 defects, all fixed in this same commit.

KNOWN GAP handed to S4, not closed here: only the '## Removed Requirements' heading sets status=removed. The inline '**REMOVED** by' marker this repo actually uses (SPEC 2.5, 01-adopted/spec.md:198) is unrecognised, so an inline-removed row parses as ACTIVE and would hit the duplicate rule as the hardest collision form. Pre-existing parser behaviour; S4 (one header-driven parser) owns it.

surface_verification.note: The behavioural surface is the manifest key form plus the blast radius of refusing to write it. Four suites drive it: the contract gate (rename survival, key/id agreement, duplicate fail-closed, frozen fixture + disarm detector), the Group-D ambiguity guard, the RTM layer columns, and the update_compliance loop guard. Scoped to ONE plugin tree because mixing plugin and shared test dirs in a single pytest run trips the conftest name collision (exit 3).

behaviors.behavior: namespace derives from the FR id's group digits
no call site can pass a path-derived namespace
a non-canonical id has no derivable namespace (no invented key)
every requirement's inner fields are unchanged vs v2
behaviors.evidence: empirical probe: regenerated the real monorepo manifest and compared node-by-node against the committed v2 one across id/tests/coverage/required_layers/required_layers_source/title/status/priority/spec_path -> zero differences, while all 15 keys moved

behaviors.behavior: schema_version is 3 and travels with MODEL_VERSION
a frozen v3 contract fixture exists and matches the live manifest
published fixtures are frozen against origin/main
behaviors.evidence: TestTheGate::test_published_fixtures_are_frozen_against_main, plus a negative-control probe: modifying a published fixture returns a reason (ARMED); restoring it returns None

test_traceability_contract.py::test_keys_survive_a_split_directory_rename (two fixture trees differing only in directory name) + test_rtm_layer_columns.py::test_row_resolves_after_its_split_directory_is_renamed

behaviors.behavior: spec_path still records where the row was found
golden corpus diffs are explained; id-only targets do not move
behaviors.evidence: regen_golden.py --check: 38 removals of the "namespace" cell in the single target parse._requirement_parse.parse_requirements, zero additions, and zero spec_path or split cells moved -- so no id-only target changed.

behaviors.behavior: two specs claiming one v3 key fail closed instead of dropping a requirement
a key whose namespace disagrees with its node id is rejected at write time
behaviors.evidence: test_traceability_contract.py::TestKeyAgreesWithItsNodeId plus an end-to-end probe through _validate_manifest rejecting both 01-adopted::FR-03.01 and the shape-valid-but-wrong 02::FR-03.01

behaviors.behavior: a tombstone row alongside a live row is NOT a collision, and the ACTIVE node survives regardless of spec discovery order
one collector refusing to write does not dark the other reports
behaviors.evidence: test_update_compliance_phases.py::test_a_duplicate_fr_id_does_not_abort_the_other_reports -- drives the real CLI: exit 1 + generator_errors naming test_links, while change-history, sbom and dashboard still write

behaviors.behavior: both integrity errors escape _layer_coverage_regen's except (OSError, ValueError) so F11 names the real cause
behaviors.evidence: test_traceability_contract.py::TestIntegrityErrorsReachTheOperator -- exercises the literal swallow clause, not just the class hierarchy

behaviors.behavior: the ambiguity-guard fixture is schema-VALID, so the path it pins is reachable through load_manifest
behaviors.evidence: test_group_d_hardening.py::test_the_collided_fixture_is_schema_valid_so_the_guard_is_reachable -- the old '02-b::' key failed propertyNames, so load_manifest returned None and D-layer/D-orphan SKIPped while the tests passed

behaviors.behavior: the contract gate cannot silently disarm itself by a renamed CONTRACTS_DIR/STEM, and the bump SIZE is enforced
behaviors.evidence: test_traceability_contract.py::test_the_shape_change_forces_the_bump_it_obliges (CE.require_bump + a stem-scoped disarm detector); probe confirms the detector FIRES for a stem published on main but absent under the configured constants
